description: consolidated city-county in Muscogee County, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Columbus is a consolidated city-county located on the west-central border of the U.S. state of Georgia.

description: president of the United States from 1953 to 1961 (1890–1969)
description_wiki: Dwight David "Ike" Eisenhower (born David Dwight Eisenhower; October 14, 1890 – March 28, 1969) was the 34th president of the United States, serving from 1953 to 1961.

description: railroad constructed to join Macon, Georgia, and Savannah, Georgia
description_wiki: The Central of Georgia Railway (reporting mark CG; abbreviated CofG) was a railroad in Georgia, United States, that was active from 1895–1963.

description: Journalist, war correspondent, novelist, short story writer (1864–1916)
description_wiki: Richard Harding Davis (April 18, 1864 – April 11, 1916) was an American journalist and writer of fiction and drama, known foremost as the first American war correspondent to cover the Spanish–American War, the Second Boer War, and World War I. His writing greatly assisted the political career of Theodore Roosevelt.

description: private university in New Haven, Connecticut
description_wiki: Yale University is a private Ivy League research university in New Haven, Connecticut, United States.
Frank Merriwell is a fictional character appearing in a series of novels and short stories by Gilbert Patten, who wrote under the pseudonym Burt L. Standish.
Jed Harris (born Jacob Hirsch Horowitz; February 25, 1900 – November 15, 1979) was an Austrian-born American theatrical producer and director.

aliases: University of California at Los Angeles
State Normal School at Los Angeles
description: public university in Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) is a public land-grant research university in the Westwood neighborhood of Los Angeles, California, United States.

description: private university in Los Angeles, California, United States
description_wiki: The University of Southern California (USC, SC, or Southern Cal[a]) is a private research university in Los Angeles, California, United States.
Harvard University is a private Ivy League research university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States.

aliases: The Chancellor, Masters, and Scholars of the University of Cambridge
description: collegiate public research university in Cambridge, England, United Kingdom
description_wiki: The University of Cambridge is a collegiate public research university in Cambridge, England.

description: collegiate research university in Oxford, England
description_wiki: The University of Oxford is a collegiate research university in Oxford, England.
Frank Fabian Mankiewicz II  (May 16, 1924 – October 23, 2014) was an American journalist, political adviser, president of National Public Radio, and public relations executive.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.
